us-politics.headline: Iran Rejects US Proposal as Hormuz Deadline Looms
us-politics.body:
Iran dismissed a US ceasefire proposal as 'unrealistic' ahead of Trump's self-imposed deadline for the country to open the Strait of Hormuz. Trump held press conferences Monday to address the standoff, signaling he is prepared to escalate. A draft ceasefire proposal has reportedly been circulated, but Tehran's rejection leaves the path forward unclear.

JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon warned that a war with Iran could tip the US economy into recession, citing inflation risks and energy market disruption. Opinion writers have raised separate alarms about nuclear decision-making, arguing that war powers of this magnitude should not rest with a single person. The standoff is shaping up as one of the most consequential foreign policy tests of Trump's second term.

sources.title: Live updates: Trump to hold press conferences as deadline nears for Iran to open Strait of Hormuz
Iran spurns ‘unrealistic’ US peace proposal
Watch live: Trump briefs press ahead of deadline on Iran
US, Iran receive draft ceasefire proposal: Reports
Dimon warns Iran war could sink economy into a recession
Nuclear war shouldn’t come down to just one person — especially when it’s Trump

us-politics.headline: Supreme Court Clears Way for Bannon Conviction Dismissal
us-politics.body:
The Supreme Court sided with Steve Bannon Monday, clearing the path for his contempt of Congress conviction to be dismissed. The ruling follows a Trump pardon and applies similar logic to a separate case involving a former Cincinnati city councilman also pardoned by Trump. The court did not rule on the underlying merits of either contempt charge.

Critics argue the decisions set a troubling precedent for congressional oversight power. Supporters say the pardons were lawful exercises of executive authority and the courts are simply following established procedure. The rulings add to a broader pattern of Trump-era legal cases being unwound through the pardon power.

sources.title: Supreme Court sides with Steve Bannon in bid to dismiss Jan. 6 conviction
Supreme Court clears the way for Bannon contempt case to be dismissed
Supreme Court paves way for dismissal of Steve Bannon contempt of Congress case
Supreme Court clears way for dismissal in case of ex-Cincinnati City Council member pardoned by Trump

us-politics.headline: Democrats Target State Legislatures, Eye Midterm Advantage
us-politics.body:
Democrats announced their first slate of state legislative targets ahead of November, with the DLCC identifying Republican-held seats it believes are in play. Analysts writing in The Hill argue Democrats still hold a structural edge heading into the midterms, pointing to historical patterns of opposition-party gains and Republican headwinds on policy.

A separate challenge is emerging on the media front. Democratic strategists are grappling with the growing influence of right-leaning podcasters, a space Republicans have dominated since 2024. Some argue the party has been slow to adapt its communications strategy to meet voters where they now get their news.

sources.title: Democrats announce first slate of state legislative targets ahead of November
Democrats still have the edge heading into the Midterms
Democrats face new reality: Podcasters’ political power

us-politics.headline: Trump Endorses Hilton, Offshore Drilling Rules Rolled Back
us-politics.body:
Trump endorsed former Fox News host Steve Hilton in the California governor's race, injecting a high-profile name into a contest that could test Republican competitiveness in the nation's largest state. Hilton would face a steep climb in a heavily Democratic electorate. Meanwhile, California's high-speed rail project drew fresh scrutiny after an official admitted costs have ballooned to $126 billion with no tracks yet laid.

On the regulatory front, the Trump administration moved to recombine two federal offshore drilling agencies that were separated after the 2010 Deepwater Horizon disaster. Environmental groups warned the merger weakens safety oversight. Supporters say it streamlines permitting and reduces bureaucratic duplication.

sources.title: Trump endorses former Fox News host Steve Hilton in California governor race
Newsom's California rail project now expected to cost $126B, official admits, with still no tracks laid
Trump administration recombining two federal agencies separated after Gulf oil spill

au-politics.headline: Fuel Crisis Bites Tourism, Commuters as Costs Spiral
au-politics.body:
Soaring fuel prices are reshaping how Australians move and travel. Sydney commuters are abandoning cars for bikes in growing numbers, citing costs they describe as unsustainable. The shift is visible on city roads and in cycling retail.

Regional tourism is hurting badly. An outback Queensland roadhouse that typically draws Easter crowds reported just two visitors over the long weekend. Operators say the fuel price pinch is keeping travellers close to home, threatening businesses that depend on holiday traffic.

No government response has been announced. The squeeze is hitting both urban and remote communities, though in very different ways.

sources.title: Cars make way for bikes as Sydney commuters saddle up to circumvent ‘crazy’ fuel costs
Outback tourist pub had two visitors this Easter amid fuel price pinch

au-politics.headline: AI in Environmental Reviews Draws Robodebt Comparisons
au-politics.body:
Scientists are warning that using artificial intelligence to prepare and assess environmental impact statements risks repeating the failures of the robodebt scheme. Their concern is that automated systems will produce confident-sounding but flawed conclusions, with real consequences for ecosystems and communities before errors are caught.

The robodebt comparison is pointed. That scheme used automated data-matching to issue welfare debt notices, many of them wrong, causing serious harm before courts intervened. Researchers argue environmental assessments carry similar risks if human expert judgment is removed from the process.

The warning comes as governments look to speed up approval processes. Critics say cutting corners on scrutiny is precisely what makes large-scale automated failures possible.

sources.title: Using AI to prepare and evaluate environmental assessments risks ‘robodebt-style’ failures, scientists say

au-politics.headline: Cyclone Maila Threatens Far North Queensland Again
au-politics.body:
A second severe tropical cyclone is bearing down on far north Queensland just three weeks after Cyclone Narelle caused significant damage to the region. Cyclone Maila has been upgraded to severe status, raising fears that communities still recovering from Narelle have little time to prepare.

Back-to-back cyclones in the same area within weeks is unusual and stretches emergency resources. Authorities have not yet confirmed a precise landfall location, but the far north coast is in the projected path. Residents are being urged to monitor warnings closely.

The timing compounds an already difficult recovery period for affected towns and infrastructure.

sources.title: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila could hit far north Queensland three weeks after Narelle tore through

au-politics.headline: Jury Trial Debate and Hanson Preferences Stir Political Commentary
au-politics.body:
Conservative commentators are raising two distinct political concerns this Easter Monday. The Spectator has published a piece arguing that moves to scrap jury trials in certain cases represent a serious erosion of civil liberties, warning that efficiency-driven court reforms carry long-term democratic costs.

Separately, commentary on Pauline Hanson and One Nation's preference flows is circulating ahead of the federal election cycle. Right-leaning analysts argue Hanson's positioning gives her outsized influence over preference deals, while critics question whether major parties should accept or direct One Nation preferences at all.

The two debates reflect broader tensions on the right between libertarian instincts and electoral pragmatism. Neither major party has responded publicly to the commentary.

sources.title: Scrapping jury trials will cost us all
Pauline Hanson: straight to number one

world.headline: US-Israel War on Iran Escalates as Ceasefire Talks Stall
world.body:
US and Israeli strikes killed 34 people and hit Iran's top university, power plants, pharmaceutical producers, and civilian bridges over the past 24 hours. The attacks are gutting civilian infrastructure - bombing pharma facilities threatens patients dependent on domestic drug supply, while power plant strikes risk widespread blackouts. Iran has responded by threatening to close the Bab al-Mandeb strait, a chokepoint carrying roughly 10% of global trade.

Pakistan floated a two-phase truce proposal, but Iran rejected any deal that requires keeping the Strait of Hormuz open under a temporary ceasefire. Trump complicated diplomacy further by publicly claiming the US armed Iranian dissidents through Kurdish intermediaries - Kurdish groups denied it. A separate US special operations mission rescued an American air officer from inside Iran, raising the operational tempo on the ground.

sources.title: Where are Iran’s power plants that Trump has threatened to destroy?
In maps and photos: Five Iranian civilian bridges at risk of US strikes
Bombing of Iran's pharma producers threatens patients' lives
Has Trump confirmed Iran’s claim that protesters were US-armed?

How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
Iran’s top university bombed as US, Israel intensify attacks; 34 killed

world.headline: Israel Pounds Lebanon, Displacing Over a Million People
world.body:
Israeli airstrikes have flattened towns across southern Lebanon and hit areas around Beirut previously designated safe zones, forcing up to 1.2 million people to flee. Families are sheltering in mountain villages with little infrastructure to absorb the displacement. The scale of destruction mirrors the early weeks of the Gaza campaign.

Yemen's Houthis have formally entered the conflict on Iran's side, deepening fears among Yemeni civilians of intensified US and Israeli retaliation against their already war-battered country. Gaza's economy remains strangled - Israel's blockade has left young Palestinians jobless with no path out.

sources.title: Israel destroys southern Lebanon towns, hits ‘safe’ areas around Beirut
Up to 1.2 million people forced to flee as Israel pummels Lebanon
Families fleeing Israeli attacks take refuge in Lebanon’s mountains
Yemen’s civilians fear fallout after Houthis enter Iran war
Jobless young Palestinians trapped as Israel holds Gaza’s economy hostage

world.headline: Ukraine Strikes Deep Into Russia's Energy Network
world.body:
Ukrainian forces hit the Black Sea port of Novorossiysk, a major Russian energy export hub, and continued a sustained campaign against refineries that residents of St Petersburg say they can now smell burning. The strikes mark a significant escalation in Ukraine's strategy of targeting Russia's energy revenue rather than purely frontline positions.

The refinery attacks are designed to squeeze the fuel supply that sustains Russian military logistics. Russia has not publicly confirmed the extent of the damage.

sources.title: Ukraine strikes Russian Black Sea energy hub Novorossiysk
‘Smell’ of war comes to St Petersburg as Ukraine hammers Russian refineries

world.headline: Myanmar Junta Chief Becomes President; Bangladesh Measles Crisis
world.body:
Myanmar's military ruler Min Aung Hlaing has been formally elected president, consolidating power more than four years after the 2021 coup. The move signals the junta intends to legitimise its rule through a controlled political process rather than return to civilian government. Regional observers expect little to change for the millions displaced by the civil war.

Separately, Bangladesh declared a health emergency after 100 children died of suspected measles within a single month, prompting an emergency vaccination drive. DR Congo agreed to accept deportees from the United States under a deal with the Trump administration.

sources.title: Myanmar junta chief elected president — what comes next?
DR Congo agrees to take deportees from the US
Emergency jabs after 100 children die of suspected measles in a month in Bangladesh

ai.headline: AI Propaganda Goes Viral, and It's Getting Sophisticated
ai.body:
A coordinated pro-Iran campaign used Lego-themed videos to spread political messaging across social platforms, offering a concrete example of what analysts are calling a new era of AI-assisted propaganda. The campaign blended innocuous aesthetics with targeted narratives, making content harder to flag and easier to share.

The broader pattern is alarming. AI tools now let small, underfunded actors produce high-volume, emotionally resonant content that spreads before fact-checkers can respond. Virality itself becomes the message - reach signals legitimacy to casual viewers. Researchers warn that the bottleneck is no longer production but detection, and detection is losing.

sources.title: When Virality Is the Message: The New Age of AI Propaganda
The Team Behind a Pro-Iran, Lego-Themed Viral-Video Campaign

ai.headline: ChatGPT Gets Real-World Hooks via DoorDash, Uber, Spotify
ai.body:
OpenAI rolled out app integrations letting ChatGPT users order food, book rides, and control music without leaving the chat interface. The move pushes ChatGPT closer to an operating layer for daily tasks rather than a standalone question-answering tool.

The integrations signal OpenAI's ambition to own the user session, not just the query. By embedding commerce and services directly, it competes less with Google Search and more with super-app models like WeChat. Whether users actually route these tasks through a chatbot remains the open question.

sources.title: How to use the new ChatGPT app integrations, including DoorDash, Spotify, Uber, and others

ai.headline: Microsoft Calls Copilot Entertainment. Lawyers Are Watching.
ai.body:
Microsoft's terms of service quietly classify Copilot as being for entertainment purposes only, a legal hedge that strips users of any reasonable-reliance argument if the AI gives bad advice. The framing contradicts how Microsoft markets the product to businesses and professionals.

It's a familiar pattern. Companies deploy AI aggressively in high-stakes contexts while burying liability disclaimers in fine print. As enterprise adoption grows, the gap between marketing claims and legal protections is becoming a serious regulatory target.

sources.title: Copilot is ‘for entertainment purposes only,’ according to Microsoft’s terms of use

ai.headline: Spain's Xoople Raises $130M to Feed AI Earth Data
ai.body:
Barcelona-based Xoople closed a $130 million Series B to expand its geospatial mapping platform, which processes satellite and sensor data into structured datasets for AI training and inference. The raise reflects surging demand for high-quality real-world data as model builders exhaust conventional text corpora.

Geospatial data is increasingly strategic. Logistics, climate modeling, defense, and urban planning all depend on accurate, continuously updated Earth observation. Xoople is betting that mapping the physical world at scale becomes foundational infrastructure for the next wave of AI applications.

sources.title: Spain’s Xoople raises $130 million Series B to map the Earth for AI

btc.headline: Bitcoin Reclaims $69K as Iran Ceasefire Talks Spark Rally
btc.body:
Bitcoin climbed back above $69,000 Monday after reports of Iran ceasefire negotiations triggered a short squeeze and lifted broader crypto markets. Geopolitical tension had been weighing on prices, and the sudden shift in sentiment caught leveraged bears off guard. Prediction markets had been tracking Iran war probabilities in real time, effectively functioning as macro indicators for traders.

Analysts remain cautious. One warned that without a sustained reclaim of $75,000, a drop toward $10,000 stays on the table. NEAR Protocol led altcoin gains with an 8.1% weekend jump, while XRP edged to $1.33 but stayed range-bound. The week ahead keeps inflation data in focus as a key test for the rally's durability.

sources.title: CoinDesk 20 performance update: NEAR Protocol (NEAR) jumps 8.1% over weekend
Bitcoin has room to rally, but there's a catch
Bitcoin rallies on report of Iran ceasefire talks, Algorand extends gains
First real bull signal since 2025? Five things to know in Bitcoin this week
Iran war bets turn prediction markets into real-time macro radar: Sygnum
Inflation takes center stage: Crypto Week Ahead
XRP drifts higher to $1.33, but range-bound trade still dominates
Bitcoin reclaims $69,000 as ceasefire talks surface and crypto shorts get squeezed
Bitcoin meltdown to $10,000 remains likely unless prices reclaim $75,000, analyst says
Trump-Iran deadline chaos sends crypto higher while ceasefire hopes rise

btc.headline: Strategy Buys $330M More Bitcoin Despite $14.5B Paper Loss
btc.body:
Strategy added 4,871 bitcoin for roughly $330 million last week, pushing its total holdings to nearly 767,000 BTC. The purchase came after a brief pause that had prompted Michael Saylor to hint publicly at an imminent buy. The firm's Q1 paper losses topped $14.5 billion, yet management shows no sign of slowing accumulation.

Separately, a solo miner beat 1-in-28,000 odds to mine a block and collect a $210,000 reward, a reminder that the network's proof-of-work lottery still occasionally pays out to small operators. Bitmine also made headlines, disclosing an ether treasury of 4.8 million ETH and announcing its stock will move to the NYSE.

sources.title: Solo bitcoin miner overcomes 1-in-28,000 odds to secure $210,000 block reward
Strategy adds $330M BTC as paper losses top $14.5B in Q1
Bitmine's ether treasury hits 4.8 million ETH as stock listing moves to NYSE
Strategy added another 4,871 bitcoin for $330 million, with holdings nearing 767,000 BTC
Michael Saylor hints at next Strategy Bitcoin buy after weeklong pause

btc.headline: Circle Launches Quantum-Resistant Blockchain as Mow Urges Caution
btc.body:
Circle unveiled Arc, a new layer-1 blockchain built with quantum-resistant cryptography from launch. The stablecoin issuer is positioning Arc as infrastructure ready for a post-quantum world, a bet that the threat is close enough to warrant building defenses in now rather than retrofitting them later.

Not everyone agrees with the urgency. Samson Mow warned that rushing a quantum fix onto Bitcoin could backfire, arguing that a poorly implemented patch poses more immediate risk than quantum computers themselves. The debate reflects a broader tension in the industry between acting early and acting carefully on a threat that remains years from being practical.

sources.title: Rushed quantum fix may backfire for Bitcoin, Samson Mow warns
Circle future-proofs Arc blockchain against quantum computing threats
Circle unveils quantum-resistant roadmap for its layer-1 blockchain Arc

btc.headline: North Korean Infiltrators, Deepfake KYC Tools Raise DeFi Security Alarm
btc.body:
A cybersecurity researcher revealed that North Korean operatives have been embedding themselves inside DeFi protocols since the 2020 DeFi summer, working on codebases across countless projects over seven years. The scale and duration of the infiltration is larger than most in the industry had publicly acknowledged.

A separate threat has also emerged: a new AI-powered cybercrime tool is targeting crypto platforms and banks by using deepfakes to defeat KYC identity checks. Together, the two developments highlight how security vulnerabilities in crypto are increasingly human and social rather than purely technical. China also ordered Apple to remove Jack Dorsey's encrypted messaging app Bitchat from its App Store, citing the app's use during Iran protests.

sources.title: New AI cybercrime tool targets crypto, bank KYC systems via deepfakes
China orders Apple to pull Dorsey's Bitchat, the messaging app used during Iran protests
North Korean workers have been infiltrating DeFi for 7 years: Researcher
Apple pulls Jack Dorsey's Bitchat from China at Beijing’s request

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, caramelised white fish marinated in sweet miso and mirin, broiled until lacquered and just flaking
food.tip: Marinate the cod for at least 24 hours — the longer the miso sits on the fish, the deeper the umami crust you'll get under the grill

exercise.name: Monday Reset: Loaded Carry Circuit
exercise.description: Cycle through farmer's carries, suitcase carries, and overhead carries across a set distance, resting only between variations — brutal for grip, core, and posture after a weekend of sitting

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same object — philosophers have extended this to human cells, memory, and consciousness, spawning centuries of debate about what makes you *you*.
learn.why: On a Monday at the start of a new week it's a quietly destabilising hour well spent — Derek Parfit's modern take in *Reasons and Persons* will make you question whether the person who went to sleep Sunday night is strictly the same one reading this now
